name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَبِٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَمَا هُم بِمُؤْمِنِينَ (٨)
```

```english
Among the people are those who say, ‘We have faith in Allah and the Last Day,’ but they have no faith. (8)
```

```arabic
يُخَـٰدِعُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَمَا يَخْدَعُونَ إِلَّآ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ (٩)
```

```english
They seek to deceive Allah and those who have faith, yet they deceive no one but themselves, but they are not aware. (9)
```

```arabic
فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ فَزَادَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مَرَضًا ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْذِبُونَ (١٠)
```

```english
There is a sickness in their hearts; then Allah increased their sickness, and there is a painful punishment for them because of the lies they used to tell. (10)
```

```arabic
وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَا تُفْسِدُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُصْلِحُونَ (١١)
```

```english
When they are told, ‘Do not cause corruption on the earth,’ they say, ‘We are only reformers!’ (11)
```

```arabic
أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْسِدُونَ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا يَشْعُرُونَ (١٢)
```

```english
Behold! They are themselves the agents of corruption, but they are not aware. (12)
```

```arabic
وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ ءَامِنُوا۟ كَمَآ ءَامَنَ ٱلنَّاسُ قَالُوٓا۟ أَنُؤْمِنُ كَمَآ ءَامَنَ ٱلسُّفَهَآءُ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلسُّفَهَآءُ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا يَعْلَمُونَ (١٣)
```

```english
And when they are told, ‘Believe like the people who have believed,’ they say, ‘Shall we believe like the fools who have believed?’ Behold! They are themselves the fools, but they do not know. (13)
```

```arabic
وَإِذَا لَقُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ شَيَـٰطِينِهِمْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُسْتَهْزِءُونَ (١٤)
```

```english
When they meet the faithful, they say, ‘We believe,’ but when they are alone with their devils, they say, ‘We are with you; we were only deriding [them].’ (14)
```

```arabic
ٱللَّهُ يَسْتَهْزِئُ بِهِمْ وَيَمُدُّهُمْ فِى طُغْيَـٰنِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ (١٥)
```

```english
It is Allah who derides them, and leaves them bewildered in their rebellion. (15)
```

```arabic
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱشْتَرَوُا۟ ٱلضَّلَـٰلَةَ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ فَمَا رَبِحَت تِّجَـٰرَتُهُمْ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُهْتَدِينَ (١٦)
```

```english
They are the ones who bought error for guidance, so their trade did not profit them, nor were they guided. (16)
```

```arabic
مَثَلُهُمْ كَمَثَلِ ٱلَّذِى ٱسْتَوْقَدَ نَارًا فَلَمَّآ أَضَآءَتْ مَا حَوْلَهُۥ ذَهَبَ ٱللَّهُ بِنُورِهِمْ وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتٍ لَّا يُبْصِرُونَ (١٧)
```

```english
Their parable is that of one who lighted a torch, and when it had lit up all around him, Allah took away their light, and left them sightless in a manifold darkness. (17)
```

```arabic
صُمٌّۢ بُكْمٌ عُمْىٌ فَهُمْ لَا يَرْجِعُونَ (١٨)
```

```english
Deaf, dumb, and blind, they will not come back. (18)
```

```arabic
أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فِيهِ ظُلُمَـٰتٌ وَرَعْدٌ وَبَرْقٌ يَجْعَلُونَ أَصَـٰبِعَهُمْ فِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِم مِّنَ ٱلصَّوَٰعِقِ حَذَرَ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ مُحِيطٌۢ بِٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (١٩)
```

```english
Or that of a rainstorm from the sky, wherein is darkness, thunder and lightning: they put their fingers in their ears due to the thunderclaps, apprehensive of death; and Allah besieges the faithless. (19)
```

```arabic
يَكَادُ ٱلْبَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَـٰرَهُمْ ۖ كُلَّمَآ أَضَآءَ لَهُم مَّشَوْا۟ فِيهِ وَإِذَآ أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ قَامُوا۟ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (٢٠)
```

```english
The lightning almost snatches away their sight: whenever it shines for them, they walk in it, and when the darkness falls upon them, they stand. Had Allah willed, He would have taken away their hearing and sight. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (20)
```

## (Commentary)

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ** (And among the people are those who say...) until the end of the verses. Deception (الخدعة) is a type of cunning (المكر), and the devil (الشيطان) is the wicked one (الشرير); for this reason, Iblis was named *Shaytan*.

In these verses is an exposition of the state of the hypocrites (المنافقين) — *those who outwardly profess faith while concealing disbelief* — and, if Allah wills, a detailed discussion regarding them will follow in *Surah al-Munafiqun* and elsewhere.

**And His saying, the Exalted:** **مَثَلُهُمْ كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْقَدَ نَارًا** — **Their parable is that of one who lighted a torch, and when it illuminated around him, Allah took away their light and left them in a manifold darkness in which they cannot see.** (2:17). This is a parable (مثل) struck to describe their condition: they are like one who has fallen into a blind darkness (ظلمة عمياء) where good cannot be distinguished from evil, nor the beneficial from the harmful. Thus, he seeks to dispel it by some means of illumination, such as a fire he kindles to see his surroundings. Yet, once it is kindled and illuminates what is around him, Allah extinguishes it through some cause, such as wind, rain, or the like, leaving him as he was before.

He remains in darkness and becomes entangled between two darknesses: the darkness he was originally in, and the darkness of confusion (الحيرة) and the futility of the means he sought.

This is the state of the hypocrite; he manifests faith (الإيمان) and thereby benefits from some of the advantages of the religion (الدين) by sharing with the believers in their inheritances, marriages, and other matters. However, when the time of death arrives—which is the moment when the full benefit of faith should be realized—Allah takes away his light, nullifies what he has done, and leaves him in a darkness where he perceives nothing. He falls between his original darkness and the darkness he brought upon himself by his own deeds.

**And His saying, the Exalted:** **أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ** — **Or like a rainstorm from the sky, wherein is darkness, thunder and lightning. They put their fingers in their ears against the thunderclaps, being afraid of death; and Allah besets the faithless.** (2:19). The rainstorm (الصيب) is heavy rain; lightning (البرق) is well-known; thunder (الرعد) is the sound produced from the clouds during lightning; and the thunderbolt (الصاعقة) is that which descends from the lightning.

This is a second parable struck to describe the state of the hypocrites in their outward display of faith. They are like one caught in a heavy rainstorm from the sky, accompanied by a darkness that robs him of sight and discernment. The rainstorm compels him to flee and seek escape, yet the darkness prevents him from doing so. Terrifying elements like thunder and thunderbolts surround him, so he finds no recourse but to make use of the lightning and its glow. However, this light is neither permanent nor continuous; whenever it shines for him, he walks, and when it turns dark over him, he stands still.

Such is the state of the hypocrite: he does not love faith, yet he finds no alternative but to manifest it. Due to the lack of correspondence (المواطأة) between his heart and his tongue, his path is not fully illuminated for him. Consequently, he continues to stumble time and again, and trips repeatedly; he walks a little and stands still a little. Allah exposes him through this, and had Allah willed, He would have taken away his hearing and his sight, exposing him from the very first day.
